statement: Bounded scheduler dispatch causes queue-age growth under concurrent windows and can look like stalling without violating correctness.
rationale: Per-tick caps and conditional rescheduling are expected to trade throughput for safety.
keyAssumptions: startScheduler is called reliably when new work appears
lock contention does not prevent sustained progress
validationCriteria: Queue age p50/p95 increases during burst then drains
No process remains terminally pending after backlog clears
critiquePoints: Could be a hidden scheduler kickoff bug rather than expected backpressure
questions: What queue-age threshold should trigger auto-heal or alerting?
